title: An extension of the theory of planned behavior to predict pedestrians' violating crossing behavior using structural equation modeling
tasks: Predicting pedestrian violating crossing behavior intention
datasets: Survey data from 260 respondents in Dalian, China
claims: Instrumental attitude is a significant predictor of pedestrians' violating crossing behavior intention across all models tested.
Descriptive norm (perceived actual behavior of family and friends) is a stronger predictor than subjective norm (perceived approval) and remains significant when added to the basic TPB model.

Conformity tendency (the tendency to modify behavior when around others) is a strong predictor of violating crossing intention, indicating that the presence of other pedestrians influences behavioral intention.
